courses.name: Introduction to African Studies I
courses.description: Introduction to African studies, including history, geography, literature, and the arts.

courses.name: Introduction to African Studies II
courses.description: Introduction to contemporary political, economic, and social dimensions of Africa.

courses.name: The Horn of Africa
courses.description: The economic, social and political challenges facing the Horn of Africa, placing them in historical and global context.  These countries may be discussed: Djibouti, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Somalia, Sudan, South Sudan.

courses.name: The Great Lakes Region of Africa
courses.description: The economic, social and political challenges facing the Great Lake Regions of Africa, including the 1994 Rwanda genocide and its aftermath. These countries may be discussed: Burundi, Democratic Republic of Congo, Kenya, Rwanda, Tanzania, Uganda.

courses.name: North Africa
courses.description: The economic, social and political challenges facing Egypt and the Maghreb countries of North Africa, including the “Arab Spring”.  These countries may be discussed: Algeria, Egypt, Libya, Morocco, Mauritania, Tunisia, Western Sahara.

courses.name: West Africa
courses.description: The economic, social and political challenges facing countries of West Africa, including domestic issues and regional relations.  These countries may be discussed: Benin, Burkina Faso, Cape Verde, Cote d’Ivoire, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea Bissau, Liberia, Mali, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Togo.

courses.name: Southern Africa
courses.description: The economic, social and political challenges facing the  countries of southern Africa, including the legacies of apartheid. These countries may be discussed: Angola, Botswana, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Mauritius, Mozambique, Namibia, Seychelles, South Africa, Swaziland, Tanzania, Zambia, Zimbabwe.

courses.name: Globalization and Popular Culture in Africa
courses.description: This course examines new popular life-worlds in Africa. Though potentially "elusive" to conceptualize, this course shows how these forms of popular culture are related to the role of youth culture and social media in an age of globalization and democratization.

courses.name: Regions in Africa: Cultures, Society, Politics
courses.description: Using dominant linguistic borderlines that have shaped much of the African experience in the last century, this course will look at themes cutting across culture, geography, society and politics in francophone, anglophone, lusophone and arabophone Africa.

courses.name: African Social and Political Thought
courses.description: The African communitarian tradition.  Contemporary African social and political thought, situated in their broad historical contexts.

courses.name: The African City
courses.description: Historical emergence and contemporary issues of the African city.

courses.name: African Migrations and Diasporas
courses.description: Movements of African peoples, from the slave trade era to the present.  African diaspora communities around the world and their relationship with Africa.

courses.name: Special Topics in African Studies
courses.description: A special topic related to African Studies, through one or more disciplinary lenses.  Course content will vary from year to year.

courses.name: African Studies Abroad: Selected Topics
courses.description: Based at one of Carleton's partner universities in Africa, course will include lectures, seminars, guest speakers, field visits and group research projects to examine a topic in African studies, as selected by the instructor.  Topic and location may change annually.

courses.name: Thematic Topic
courses.description: A special topic that takes a thematic approach to African Studies. Course content will vary from year to year.

courses.name: African Cinema
courses.description: Major moments, debates, figures and movements in African cinema around such categories as the colonial, the anti-colonial, the postcolonial, the national, the continental, the diasporic, the global, race, Afro-futurism, and world cinema, interrogating in the process the very category of “African cinema.”.

courses.name: Placement
courses.description: Placement for one term with an African focus.

courses.name: Spoken Word Poetry Workshop
courses.description: This intermediate-level workshop-based course explores traditions of spoken words poetry while requiring students to create and perform their own spoken word poems.

courses.name: Advanced Topics in African Studies
courses.description: Seminar examining a specialized topic in African studies.  The topic will vary from year to year.

courses.name: History of 'The African Child'
courses.description: Students will analyze the history of the figure of 'the African child' using a range of visual, sources from colonial officials, anthropologists, historians, advertisers, charity and development workers, and African children themselves.

courses.name: Comparative Indigenous Knowledge and Entrepreneurship
courses.description: Past and contemporary interconnections between Indigenous knowledge and entrepreneurship on a comparative basis. Distinguishing features of Indigenous entrepreneurship from traditional entrepreneurship such as its focus on community, connection to the land, and the role of women.

courses.name: Selected Topics in African Studies
courses.description: Selected topics in African studies not ordinarily treated in the regular course program.  The choice of topic varies from year to year.  Students should check with the institute regarding the topic offered.

courses.name: African Feminisms
courses.description: African feminisms as theoretical interventions and as political practice, and as diverse forms. Gender as a marker of power: status, hierarchy, social capability, and as a system of distribution of resources, responsibilities and solidarities.

courses.name: Tutorial in African Studies
courses.description: A tutorial on selected topics in which seminars are not available.

free_electives.degree: African Studies B.A. Combined Honours (20.0 credits)
free_electives.text: 13. Sufficient free electives to make 20.0 credits for the degree.

free_electives.degree: Specialization in Africa and Globalization B.G.In.S. Honours (20.0 credits)
Stream in Africa and Globalization B.G.In.S. (15.0 credits)
